# Phase 1: Finalize README Features

## Phase Work
- [Phase 1: Finalize README Features](07.finalize-readme-features.md) (In Progress)

---

**Status: In Progress**

## Problem Statement

The README needs to be finalized and made accurate for the current state of the extension. Several items have drifted since plans 01–06: keybindings were disabled, commands were renamed, `refreshAnchors` became a dead alias, and new settings/features were added without README updates. Additionally, the README is missing developer onboarding content, a references section, better LINK: examples, and notes on what was intentionally not ported from the original VS extension.

---

## Item 1 — Create `future-ideas.md`

**Request:** The `refreshAnchors` command exists in `package.json` and `extension.ts` but does nothing different from `scanAnchors`. Keep the code but document why it exists and where it is, so future developers aren't confused.

**Background — Scan vs Refresh:**

In the original [madskristensen/CommentsVS](https://github.com/madskristensen/CommentsVS) Visual Studio extension, the `SolutionAnchorScanner` had two distinct operations:
- **Scan** — full workspace/solution disk walk, re-reads all files, rebuilds the anchor cache from scratch
- **Refresh** — re-renders/re-displays what is already in the in-memory cache; much cheaper for cases where the filesystem hasn't changed but the view filter state has

The VS Code port never split these two operations. `refreshAnchors` was registered as a command but is simply an alias that calls `scanAnchors`:

```typescript
// extension.ts:630-633
vscode.commands.registerCommand('kat-comment-studio.refreshAnchors', () => {
  void vscode.commands.executeCommand('kat-comment-studio.scanAnchors');
});
```

It is also wired as the `requestRefresh` callback in `AnchorsGridProvider`:

```typescript
// extension.ts:393
() => { void vscode.commands.executeCommand('kat-comment-studio.refreshAnchors'); },
```

**Future potential:** If anchor scanning ever becomes slow (large monorepos), a true lightweight "refresh from cache" would apply the current scope/filter/search state to the existing in-memory `anchorCache` without touching the filesystem. This would be especially useful when scope/filter changes trigger a visual update — today those already use in-memory filtering, but having a named lightweight command vs a full disk scan would be cleaner.

**Code locations:**
- `package.json`: command definition `kat-comment-studio.refreshAnchors`
- `src/extension.ts:393` — `requestRefresh` callback wired to the grid provider
- `src/extension.ts:630-633` — command registration (alias to scanAnchors)

**Plan:** Create `.vscode/Plans/future-ideas.md` with the above explanation, then rename the command title in `package.json` to include `(OBSOLETE - SEE future-ideas.md)` so it's obvious when scanning `package.json`.

**Files changed:**
- `.vscode/Plans/future-ideas.md` (new)
- `package.json` — update `kat-comment-studio.refreshAnchors` title

---

## Item 2 — README Accuracy Review

A thorough pass to fix every inaccuracy found against the current codebase state.

### 2.1 — Keybindings are ALL disabled

In `package.json`, the keybindings section is named `"keybindings.off"` (not `"keybindings"`). This means **no keybindings are registered**:
- `Escape` → Toggle comment rendering: **NOT active**
- `Alt+PageDown` → Go to next anchor: **NOT active**
- `Alt+PageUp` → Go to previous anchor: **NOT active**

**README fixes needed:**
- **"How It Works" table** (line 38): "Press `Escape` | Toggle rendering off/on" — this keybinding is disabled. Fix the entry to reflect that Escape is not currently bound, or remove it from the table.
- **"Keyboard Shortcuts" section** (line 628–634): Lists all three shortcuts as active. Must be updated to reflect that keybindings are disabled and the user must manually add them via VS Code's keyboard shortcuts editor if desired.
- **Toggle Rendering table** (line 76–82): "Escape (in editor) | Toggle rendering off/on" row — keybinding is off.

### 2.2 — Sidebar Toolbar — "Refresh" button doesn't exist

The **Sidebar Tree View** section currently shows:
```
| 🔄 Refresh | Re-run the scan |
```
But `refreshAnchors` is **not in the `view/title` menus** in `package.json`. Only `scanAnchors` appears in the sidebar toolbar. The "Refresh" row must be removed from the toolbar table.

### 2.3 — Commands Table — refreshAnchors entry

The **Commands** section (line 611):
> `Refresh Code Anchors` | Re-scan workspace

This command is visible in the Command Palette but is identical to "Scan Code Anchors". Once `refreshAnchors` is marked obsolete in `package.json`, update this row or remove it.

### 2.4 — Export formats: no TSV

The Commands table (line 612) says:
> `Export Code Anchors` | Export to **TSV**/CSV/Markdown/JSON

The actual `anchorExporter.ts` supports only `csv | markdown | json` — **no TSV**. Fix to "CSV/Markdown/JSON".

### 2.5 — Settings table: codeLensMaxLength default

README settings table (line 588) says default is `205`. `package.json` also says `205`. ✅ No fix needed.

### 2.6 — "Cycle Rendering Mode" command was removed

Plan 06 Item 3 removed the `cycleRenderingMode` command. Verify it no longer appears anywhere in the README (it was removed in earlier cleanup — confirm it's gone).

---

## Item 3 — Add Extension Developers Section

A new `## Extension Developers` section in the README covering everything a new developer needs to work with the project.

**Contents:**

### Prerequisites
- Node.js 20+
- VS Code
- No global `vsce` install required — the `package` script uses `npx vsce`

### Getting Started
```bash
git clone <repo>
cd VS.Code.Comment.Studio
npm install
```

### Available npm Scripts

| Script | Command | Description |
|--------|---------|-------------|
| `compile` | `npm run compile` | TypeScript → JS (`tsc -p ./`). Output goes to `out/` |
| `watch` | `npm run watch` | Incremental compile in watch mode |
| `test` | `npm test` | Run all tests once (Vitest) |
| `test:watch` | `npm run test:watch` | Run tests in watch mode |
| `lint` | `npm run lint` | ESLint on `src/` |
| `package` | `npm run package` | Bump patch version, compile, and create `.vsix` |

### VS Code Tasks

Two tasks are defined in `.vscode/tasks.json`:
- **compile** (Build task) — `Ctrl+Shift+B` to run once
- **watch** (Default build, background) — runs `tsc --watch` automatically; problem matcher surfaces TypeScript errors in the Problems panel in real time

### Running / Debugging

Press **F5** (or use **Run → Start Debugging**). The `Run Extension` launch config in `.vscode/launch.json` compiles first, then opens an **Extension Development Host** window with the extension loaded.

Make changes in `src/`, save (watch compiles automatically), then press `Ctrl+R` in the Extension Development Host to reload.

### Packaging a `.vsix`

```bash
npm run package
```

This does three things:
1. Bumps the patch version in `package.json` (e.g., `1.0.0` → `1.0.1`) — file only, no git tag/commit
2. Runs `npm run compile`
3. Runs `npx vsce package` to produce `kat-comment-studio-1.0.1.vsix` (or current version)

Commit the version bump manually afterward if you want it tracked in git.

### Installing a `.vsix`

In VS Code: **Extensions** panel → `...` menu (top-right) → **Install from VSIX...** → select the file.

Or from the command line:
```bash
code --install-extension kat-comment-studio-1.0.1.vsix
```

### Running Tests

```bash
npm test
```

Tests are in `test/` and use [Vitest](https://vitest.dev/). They are pure unit tests — no VS Code host required. The test runner exits with code 0 on success.

### Project Structure

```
src/
├── extension.ts            # Activation, all command + provider registration
├── types.ts                # Shared types and interfaces
├── configuration.ts        # Settings management + .editorconfig integration
├── anchors/                # Code anchors: service, scanner, tree view, grid panel, export
├── commands/               # Comment remover commands
├── diagnostics/            # LINK: validation diagnostics
├── navigation/             # LINK: parser, navigator, validator, git service
├── parsing/                # Comment block detection, XML doc parser, language config
├── reflow/                 # Reflow engine, auto-reflow, smart paste
├── rendering/              # CodeLens provider, decoration manager/factory, prefix highlighter
└── services/               # EditorConfig service
test/                       # Vitest unit tests (mirrors src/ structure)
out/                        # Compiled JS output (git-ignored)
```

---

## Item 4 — LINK: Documentation — Workspace/SLN/Subfolder Scenarios

The existing LINK: section documents path prefixes well but doesn't explain behavior in different VS Code workspace configurations. Add a new sub-section: **"Workspace Configuration Scenarios"**.

### Key behaviors from code (`linkAnchorParser.ts`):
- `/` prefix → resolves against `workspaceFolders[0].uri.fsPath` (first workspace root)
- `@/` prefix → walks up from **the file containing the link** looking for a `.csproj`; resolves relative to that `.csproj` directory. Falls back to `workspaceFolders[0]` only when no `.csproj` exists anywhere in the ancestor chain.
- Bare paths (`Models/User.cs`) → first tries workspace folder matching, then current file's directory

### Scenarios to document:

**1. Multi-root workspace (`.code-workspace` file)**

When a `.code-workspace` file is open, VS Code exposes multiple `workspaceFolders`. The `/` prefix and `@/` fallback both use the **first folder listed** in the `.code-workspace`:

```json
// MyApp.code-workspace
{
  "folders": [
    { "path": "C:/BTR/Camelot/Core" },   ← workspaceFolders[0] — used by / and @/ fallback
    { "path": "C:/BTR/Camelot/UI" }
  ]
}
```

```
// LINK: /Services/UserService.cs   → C:/BTR/Camelot/Core/Services/UserService.cs
// LINK: @/Services/UserService.cs  → same (if current file has no .csproj parent)
```

**2. Opening a `.sln` file in VS Code**

VS Code opens the folder **containing** the `.sln` as the workspace root. It does not parse the `.sln` to discover projects — there's no special `.sln` awareness.

```
// Solution: C:/BTR/Camelot/MyApp/MyApp.sln
// Workspace root: C:/BTR/Camelot/MyApp/

// LINK: /Core/Domain/User.cs        → C:/BTR/Camelot/MyApp/Core/Domain/User.cs
// LINK: @/Services/UserService.cs   → walks up from current file for .csproj
```

If source files are inside `src/Core/` and there's a `src/Core/Core.csproj`, then `@/` resolves relative to that project root. If no `.csproj` is found in the hierarchy, falls back to the workspace root (the folder with the `.sln`).

**3. Opening a subfolder directly (no `.csproj` in hierarchy)**

```
// VS Code workspace root: C:/BTR/Camelot/Core/Domain/

// LINK: @/Models/User.cs   → falls back to workspace root
//                             = C:/BTR/Camelot/Core/Domain/Models/User.cs
// LINK: /Models/User.cs    → same (workspaceFolders[0])
```

`@/` is most useful when your source files live inside a C# project. Without a `.csproj` in the ancestor chain, it behaves the same as `/`.

## Item 5 — Add References Section

Add `## References` at the bottom of the README (above License):

```markdown
## References

- [VS Code Codicons](https://microsoft.github.io/vscode-codicons/dist/codicon.html) — icon names used in commands and UI (`$(book)`, `$(refresh)`, etc.)
- [madskristensen/CommentsVS](https://github.com/madskristensen/CommentsVS) — the original Visual Studio 2022 extension this project ports and extends
- [C# XML Documentation Tags](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/csharp/language-reference/xmldoc/recommended-tags) — official reference for all supported `<summary>`, `<param>`, `<returns>`, etc. tags
```

## Item 6 — VS Extension vs VS Code Implementation Gap Notes

The original VS extension features that were **intentionally not ported** or **differ by design**. Document these somewhere (either an "Implementation Notes" block in the developer section, or a brief note in the README intro):

| VS Extension Feature | VS Code Status |
|---|---|
| Background parallel scanning (`SolutionAnchorScanner` with threads) | Synchronous scan with VS Code progress notification. Sufficient for typical workspace sizes. |
| WPF inline adornment replacement (true inline rendering) | Replaced by CodeLens + hover popup. VS Code Decoration API cannot replace text with arbitrary HTML. |
| Double-click to edit rendered comment | Not available — VS Code decorations don't receive click events. Auto-unfold on cursor-enter is used instead. |
| Full/Compact rendering modes | Collapsed to a single "on" mode using CodeLens + hover + auto-fold. |

These differences are already partially documented in `01.port-vs-extension.md`. The README should briefly mention that this is a VS Code adaptation, not a 1:1 port, and link to the original.
